name: Grilled Peaches with Mascarpone Chantilly, Balsamic Reduction & Almond Praline
description: A refined dessert balancing caramelized peaches, airy mascarpone cream, and crunchy almond praline with a tangy balsamic finish.
keywords: peaches, grilled fruit, dessert, mascarpone, elegant

winePairings.name: Moscato d'Asti
winePairings.notes: Light sweetness and bubbles complement the peaches.

winePairings.name: Sauternes
winePairings.notes: Rich honeyed notes elevate the caramelized fruit.

steps.instruction: Prepare the balsamic reduction by simmering balsamic vinegar over low heat until reduced by half and syrupy.
steps.note: It should coat the back of a spoon.

steps.instruction: Make the almond praline by heating sugar and water until golden amber, then stir in sliced almonds.
steps.note: Spread thin and allow to cool completely.

steps.instruction: Crush the cooled praline into a mix of fine crumbs and small shards.
steps.note: Use a sieve for refined texture.

steps.instruction: Prepare mascarpone chantilly by blending mascarpone, cream, powdered sugar, and lemon zest until smooth and airy.
Mix honey, butter, vanilla, and salt, then brush onto the cut side of the peaches.
Heat a cast iron grill pan until very hot and place peaches cut-side down.
steps.note: Do not move for 3–4 minutes to develop caramelization.

steps.instruction: Flip peaches and baste with remaining honey-butter mixture.
Use a kitchen torch to lightly caramelize for a glossy finish.
Spread a swoosh of mascarpone cream on the plate using an offset spatula.
Place grilled peaches slightly off-center on the plate.
Pipe or spoon additional mascarpone cream onto the peaches.
Sprinkle almond praline crumble in a controlled, natural pattern.
Dot balsamic reduction around the plate using a squeeze bottle.
Garnish with fresh mint and a light drizzle of olive oil.
Clean plate edges and serve immediately.
